sympathetic.definition: showing understanding and care for others; also means connected by shared feeling
sympathetic.example: Sympathetic magic means believing one thing is connected to another.

barrage.definition: a large amount of something coming at you all at once
barrage.example: As soon as you announce you are pregnant, you get a barrage of advice.

announce.definition: to tell people about something publicly
announce.example: As soon as you announce you are pregnant, the advice begins.

optimal.definition: the best possible, the most perfect
optimal.example: Following all the rules may give you the impression of an optimal pregnancy.

unwittingly.example: This may unwittingly lead you to believe in a perfect parent.

impeccable.example: No one produces an impeccable child.

interfere.definition: to get in the way of something and cause problems
interfere.example: This way of thinking may interfere with your relationship.

optimized.definition: made as good or effective as possible
optimized.example: Pregnancy and parenting cannot be optimized like a project.

overbearing.definition: trying to control others in an unpleasant way
overbearing.example: Rather than giving in to overbearing perfection, relax.

perfection.definition: the state of being completely without faults
perfection.example: Impossible perfection is not the goal of parenting.

chromosomes.definition: tiny parts inside your cells that carry information about how your body looks and works
chromosomes.example: We cannot fully control what chromosomes we pass to our child.

precautions.definition: careful actions taken to prevent something bad from happening
precautions.example: Taking all the precautions can give a false feeling of control.

unpasteurized.definition: milk or food that has not been heated to kill germs
unpasteurized.example: In the UK, you are advised to avoid unpasteurized milk products.

digested.definition: ate and processed food in your body
digested.example: If you digested some before you knew you were pregnant, do not panic.

contracted.example: She worried she had contracted some illness from the food.

ghastliness.example: She feared she had contracted some ghastliness.

draconian.example: No child fits into a draconian category of perfect.

intercourse.example: Some cultures believe couples need intercourse for a successful pregnancy.

labour.definition: the process of giving birth, when the baby is being born
labour.example: Some cultures say intercourse is important even during labour.

defective.definition: not working properly, having something wrong with it
defective.example: They believed eating flying fox would make the child defective.

tremble.definition: to shake because of fear, cold, or weakness
tremble.example: They believed the child would tremble like the flying fox.

customs.definition: traditional ways of behaving that a group of people follow
customs.example: You will find such customs throughout the world.

taboos.definition: things that are not allowed by social rules or tradition
taboos.example: Every culture has its own taboos about pregnancy.

anthropologists.definition: scientists who study human cultures and societies
anthropologists.example: Anthropologists call this belief system sympathetic magic.

symptoms.definition: signs that show something is wrong with your health
symptoms.example: Symptoms in the baby are connected to what the mother ate.

lactation.definition: the time when a mother's body makes milk for her baby
lactation.example: Some rules apply during pregnancy or the lactation period.

folklore.definition: traditional stories and beliefs passed down through generations
folklore.example: Whether the rules are medical or folklore, they keep changing.

portions.definition: amounts of food served at one time
portions.example: Women who ate five or more portions of chocolate had lower risk.

pre-eclampsia.definition: a dangerous condition in pregnancy with high blood pressure
pre-eclampsia.example: Eating chocolate was linked to lower risk of pre-eclampsia.

association.definition: a connection or link between two things
association.example: The researcher studied the association between chocolate and baby behavior.

categories.definition: groups of things that are similar in some way
categories.example: The babies' behavior was rated in various categories.

soothed.example: They measured how easily soothed the babies were.

reassuring.example: Following the rules can be reassuring.

inducing.example: Not following the rules can be panic inducing.

toxic.example: Extreme toxic stress during pregnancy can affect the baby.

trauma.definition: a very shocking or painful experience that affects you deeply
trauma.example: Toxic stress caused by trauma can harm the unborn child.

adversely.definition: in a harmful or negative way
adversely.example: Extreme stress adversely affects the unborn child.

malnutrition.definition: not having enough food or the right kind of food to be healthy
malnutrition.example: Malnutrition during pregnancy can harm the baby.

foetus.definition: a baby still growing inside the mother's body (not yet born)
foetus.example: Normal stress will probably not affect your foetus.

abnormality.definition: something that is not normal in the body or health
abnormality.example: There is a risk you might have a child with an abnormality.

womb.definition: the part inside a mother's body where a baby grows
womb.example: The environment of the womb tells the baby a story.

optimism.definition: the belief that good things will happen
optimism.example: The habit of optimism helps both you and your child.

thrive.definition: to grow strong, healthy, and successful
thrive.example: Your child needs you to believe in them so they can thrive.

literary.example: She could not have written the book without her literary agent.

agent.definition: a person who represents and helps someone in business
agent.example: Her literary agent believed in her and that helped her write.

reserve.definition: to hold back and wait before deciding
reserve.example: We may like to think we reserve judgement until we know someone.

judgement.definition: an opinion you form about someone or something
judgement.example: Try to reserve judgement about your baby until you know them.

experiment.definition: a test done to discover something new
experiment.example: In the experiment, pregnant women described their baby's movements.

significant.definition: large enough to be important or noticeable
significant.example: Knowing the baby's sex made a significant difference.

vigorous.definition: strong, active, and full of energy
vigorous.example: Words used for boys were energetic and vigorous.

jabbing.definition: poking or pushing with quick sharp movements
jabbing.example: Mothers described male baby movements as jabbing.

cliched.definition: used so often that it is no longer interesting or original
cliched.example: The words followed cliched patterns about boys and girls.

lumber.definition: to burden or weigh down with something heavy or unwanted
lumber.example: Do not lumber your child with expectations before they arrive.

parasite.definition: a living thing that lives on another and takes from it
parasite.example: Thinking of your baby as a parasite will affect your relationship.

invader.definition: someone or something that comes in without being wanted
invader.example: Do not think of the baby as a wilful invader.

burden.definition: a heavy load or responsibility that is hard to carry
burden.example: Thinking of the baby as a burden will hurt your bond.

apprehensive.definition: worried or nervous about something that will happen
apprehensive.example: Are you apprehensive about meeting your baby, or looking forward to it?

gestation.definition: the time a baby spends growing inside the mother before birth
gestation.example: Babies can hear from eighteen weeks of gestation.

alleviate.definition: to make something less painful or less bad
alleviate.example: Being terrified beforehand will not alleviate grief if something goes wrong.

grief.definition: very deep sadness, especially after losing someone
grief.example: Fear will not alleviate any grief if the worst happens.
